zh.Rushton, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾盧申頓
zh.Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England: 英國坎布利其爾歐艾金頓
zh.lifeSummary: Edward Slade於大約1451年出生在英國坎布利其爾歐艾金頓。他於大約1475年與Roberta Everston在英國坎布利其爾歐艾金頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世在英國諾薩普頓史爾盧申頓。

zh-hans.Rushton, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾盧申頓
zh-hans.Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England: 英国坎布利其爾歐艾金頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Edward Slade于大约1451年出生在英国坎布利其爾歐艾金頓。他于大约1475年与Roberta Everston在英国坎布利其爾歐艾金頓结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世在英国諾薩普頓史爾盧申頓。

nameMeanings.name: Slade
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Somerset): from Middle English slade ‘valley, low-lying marshy ground’ (Old English slæd). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a valley or marsh, or habitational, for someone from any of several minor places so named: the placename Slade is especially common in Devon, where eighteen examples are known.

variants.name: Slader
meanings: English (Devon): topographic or habitational name from Middle English slade ‘valley, low-lying marshy ground’ (Old English slæd) + -er, for someone who lived in or by a valley or marsh, or at a place so named: see Slade .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Edward Slade was born about 1451, in Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England. He married Roberta Everston about 1475, in Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in Rushton, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Edward Slade est né environ 1451, à Oakington, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Roberta Everston environ 1475, à Oakington, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé à Rushton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Edward Slade wurde um 1451 in Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England geboren. Er hat um 1475 in Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England Roberta Everston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in Rushton, Northamptonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Edward Slade è nato il circa 1451 nel Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England. Lui sposato Roberta Everston circa 1475, nel Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel Rushton, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.Rushton, Northamptonshire, England: イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアルシュトン
ja.Oakington, Cambridgeshire, England: イギリスカンブリジェシュアイアオアキントン
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Sladeは約1451年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアオアキントンで生まれた。約1475年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアオアキントンでRoberta Everstonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアルシュトンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Slade(은)는 대략 1451년 영국 캄브리지실르 킨그턴에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1475년 영국 캄브리지실르 킨그턴에서 Roberta Everston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 노르섬프토실르 러슈턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Edward Slade nasceu aproximadamente 1451, em Oakington, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Roberta Everston aproximadamente 1475, em Oakington, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em Rushton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Edward Slade родился около 1451 г. в местности оакингтон, камбриджешир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Roberta Everston около 1475 г. в местности оакингтон, камбриджешир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в местности руштон, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Edward Slade nació aproximadamente 1451, en Oakington, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Roberta Everston aproximadamente 1475, en Oakington, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en Rushton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
